runTitle: Vercel Playwright MCP QA - v8.11.153 Follow-up Improvements
coveredSurfaces: Vercel production /api/version returned 8.11.153 with releaseTag v8.11.153
Vercel production /api/health?service=ai&soft=true returned healthy Cloud Run backend
Dashboard loaded in guest session with 18 servers and AI sidebar available
AI sidebar engine state showed Ready
AI Chat guidance prompt '이상감지 기능 사용법' returned guidance text instead of executing the artifact immediately
AI sidebar rendered guidance CTA button '바로 이상감지/추세 분석 실행하기'
Guidance CTA click submitted direct artifact request '전체 서버 이상감지 돌려줘'
Monitoring analysis artifact completed in the sidebar with 18 analyzed servers and 5 risk signals
Artifact card rendered MD download, JSON download, and '이상감지/추세에서 보기' actions
Current console error capture after the verified flow reported 0 errors and 0 warnings
Relevant API network calls completed with 200 responses
GitLab v8.11.153 tag pipeline deploy, deploy_ai_engine, frontend smoke, and AI Engine smoke jobs succeeded
skippedSurfaces: incident-report guidance CTA live click path: same renderer path covered by unit regression, omitted to avoid repeated live AI cost
standard five-question conversational AI pack: unchanged routing/data grounding since v8.11.147 QA, targeted run only
mobile viewport: no responsive layout change in this fix
OAuth provider login flows: guest session used for production QA
Cloud Run admin observability endpoints: not part of this guidance CTA regression
intermediate artifact timer message in production: artifact completed too quickly to observe; timer contract covered by local tests

expertAssessments.rationale: Playwright MCP verified the actual production sidebar renderer and CTA-to-artifact flow that unit tests alone missed.
GitLab tag pipeline 2525981912 succeeded across deploy, deploy_ai_engine, post_deploy_smoke, and post_deploy_ai_engine_smoke jobs; Vercel production alias is Ready.
Guidance response now stays non-executing until user clicks the CTA, then executes the intended monitoring-analysis artifact with grounded OTel data.
The completed artifact exposed the expected 18-server OpenTelemetry snapshot, 5 risk signals, role summaries, capacity alerts, and risk evidence.

usageChecks.summary: Vercel usage checked after v8.11.153 production QA; effective 8.7576 USD, billed 0.0000 USD, chargeCount 7917.

artifacts.label: Guidance CTA to monitoring artifact completion
Accessibility snapshot for verified dashboard/sidebar state

completedImprovements.title: Guidance messages render actionable CTA buttons in the production AI sidebar
completedImprovements.evidence: Playwright MCP verified guidance CTA visibility and click-through to monitoring-analysis artifact on v8.11.153.

completedImprovements.title: Guidance CTA metadata survives message transformation
completedImprovements.evidence: Regression tests and production sidebar QA verified guidanceCta metadata reaches the renderer.

completedImprovements.title: Chat artifact generation exposes staged progress message contract
completedImprovements.evidence: Local contract/unit tests verified staged timer behavior; production artifact completed before an intermediate timer step was observable.

completedImprovements.title: Top 7 line-guard follow-up targets split below 650 lines
completedImprovements.evidence: Line-guard split work completed before v8.11.151 and remained covered by root/AI Engine validation gates.

notes: Release decision: go for the tested targeted follow-up scope.
v8.11.151 initial production QA found the sidebar CTA renderer gap; v8.11.152 fixed metadata preservation; v8.11.153 fixed the actual sidebar renderer and passed the targeted production rerun.

Production CTA click completed quickly, so the intermediate G4 timer copy was not visible in live QA; this is non-blocking because the timer contract was covered locally and final artifact completion was verified.
Version evidence: /api/version returned 8.11.153, commit f65632b9442e241465a2eece47d5722a19c80603, pipeline https://gitlab.com/skyasu2/openmanager-ai/-/pipelines/2525981912.
